message.content: Count the total lines of Go code in core/pkg/tailer/. Report the count.

content.text: I'll count the total lines of Go code in the core/pkg/tailer/ directory.
Now I'll count the lines in each Go file by reading them:

content.content:
1	package tailer
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"strings"
11		"time"
12	
13		"irrlicht/core/pkg/capacity"
14	)
15	
16	// MessageEvent represents a single message event from transcript
17	type MessageEvent struct {
18		Timestamp time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
19		EventType string    `json:"event_type"`
20		Content   string    `json:"content,omitempty"`
21	}
22	
23	// SessionMetrics holds computed performance metrics
24	type SessionMetrics struct {
25		MessagesPerMinute   float64        `json:"messages_per_minute"`
26		ElapsedSeconds      int64          `json:"elapsed_seconds"`
27		LastMessageAt       time.Time      `json:"last_message_at"`
28		MessageHistory      []MessageEvent `json:"-"` // Sliding window, not serialized
29		SessionStartAt      time.Time      `json:"session_start_at"`
30		TotalTokens         int64          `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"`
31		InputTokens         int64          `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
32		OutputTokens        int64          `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
33		CacheReadTokens     int64          `json:"cache_read_tokens,omitempty"`
34		CacheCreationTokens int64          `json:"cache_creation_tokens,omitempty"`
35		// Cumulative token totals across all API turns (for cost calculation).
36		CumInputTokens         int64   `json:"cum_input_tokens,omitempty"`
37		CumOutputTokens        int64   `json:"cum_output_tokens,omitempty"`
38		CumCacheReadTokens     int64   `json:"cum_cache_read_tokens,omitempty"`
39		CumCacheCreationTokens int64   `json:"cum_cache_creation_tokens,omitempty"`
40		EstimatedCostUSD    float64        `json:"estimated_cost_usd,omitempty"`
41		ModelName           string         `json:"model_name,omitempty"`
42		ContextWindow       int64          `json:"context_window,omitempty"`
43		ContextUtilization  float64        `json:"context_utilization_percentage,omitempty"`
44		PressureLevel       string         `json:"pressure_level,omitempty"` // "safe", "caution", "warning", "critical"
45	
46		// Raw event data for real-time client-side calculations
47		TotalEventCount        int64     `json:"total_event_count,omitempty"`
48		RecentEventCount       int64     `json:"recent_event_count,omitempty"`
49		RecentEventWindowStart time.Time `json:"recent_event_window_start,omitempty"`
50	
51		// Tool call tracking — count unmatched tool_use/tool_result pairs
52		HasOpenToolCall   bool `json:"has_open_tool_call"`
53		OpenToolCallCount int  `json:"open_tool_call_count,omitempty"`
54	
55		// LastEventType is the event type of the most recent message event in
56		// the transcript (e.g. "assistant", "user", "tool_use", "tool_result").
57		// Used for content-based working/waiting detection.
58		LastEventType string `json:"last_event_type,omitempty"`
59	
60		// LastOpenToolNames holds the tool names from the most recent assistant
61		// message that called tools. Cleared when a user message appears.
62		// Used to detect user-blocking tools (AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode).
63		LastOpenToolNames []string `json:"last_open_tool_names,omitempty"`
64	
65		// LastWasUserInterrupt is true when the most recent user event was a
66		// real ESC cancellation (the exact "[Request interrupted by user]" text
67		// marker, without the "for tool use" suffix). Reset when any subsequent
68		// non-interrupt user event arrives. The classifier uses this to
69		// transition working/waiting → ready on genuine interrupts without
70		// being fooled by normal tool failures or tool denials.
71		LastWasUserInterrupt bool `json:"last_was_user_interrupt"`
72	
73		// LastWasToolDenial is true when the most recent user event was a tool
74		// denial — the user clicked "no" on a permission prompt, producing the
75		// "[Request interrupted by user for tool use]" text marker. Distinct
76		// from LastWasUserInterrupt because a denial does NOT end the agent's
77		// turn (the agent typically continues with a different approach), so
78		// it must not feed the cancellation rule. Surfaced for observability
79		// and replay-harness flicker categorization.
80		LastWasToolDenial bool `json:"last_was_tool_denial,omitempty"`
81	
82		// LastCWD is the most recent working directory seen in the transcript.
83		// Extracted during parsing so callers don't need a separate file read.
84		LastCWD string `json:"last_cwd,omitempty"`
85	
86		// LastAssistantText is the text content of the most recent assistant
87		// message, truncated to ~200 characters.
88		LastAssistantText string `json:"last_assistant_text,omitempty"`
89	
90		// PermissionMode is the session's permission mode (e.g. "default",
91		// "plan", "bypassPermissions"). Extracted from "permission-mode" events.
92		PermissionMode string `json:"permission_mode,omitempty"`
93	}
94	
95	// TranscriptTailer monitors transcript files and computes metrics.
96	// Format-specific parsing is delegated to a TranscriptParser.
97	type TranscriptTailer struct {
98		path        string
99		lastOffset  int64
100		metrics     *SessionMetrics
101		windowSize  time.Duration // Default 60 seconds
102		capacityMgr *capacity.CapacityManager
103	
104		// parser handles format-specific line parsing (Claude Code, Codex, Pi).
105		parser TranscriptParser
106	
107		// adapter name for model config fallback.
108		adapter string
109	
110		// Context window override from transcript or extended context model suffix.
111		contextWindowOverride int64
112	
113		// openToolCalls is the single source of truth for currently-open tool
114		// calls. Keyed by tool call ID; value is the tool name. Tool uses
115		// insert by ID (idempotent — duplicate IDs overwrite), tool results
116		// delete by ID (orphan IDs are harmless no-ops). HasOpenToolCall and
117		// OpenToolCallCount are derived from len(openToolCalls).
118		//
119		// Historical note: this was originally paired integer counters
120		// (toolUseCount/toolResultCount, see #102), then a []string FIFO
121		// (lastOpenToolNames, see #114). Both had the same structural weakness:
122		// no correlation between a tool_result and the tool_use it pertains to.
123		// The id-keyed map eliminates phantom entries from orphan results,
124		// duplicate tool_use events (multi-line splits), and out-of-order
125		// parallel tool closures. See issue #117.
126		openToolCalls map[string]string
127	
128		// contentChars accumulates character count from message content for
129		// token estimation when explicit token counts aren't available.
130		contentChars int64
131	
132		// Token breakdown accumulators (latest snapshot, not cumulative).
133		// Used for context utilization — always reflects the most recent API turn.
134		inputTokens         int64
135		outputTokens        int64
136		cacheReadTokens     int64
137		cacheCreationTokens int64
138	
139		// Cumulative token accumulators for cost calculation.
140		// These sum the FINAL usage from each unique API turn (requestId).
141		cumInputTokens         int64
142		cumOutputTokens        int64
143		cumCacheReadTokens     int64
144		cumCacheCreationTokens int64
145	
146		// Deduplication: track requestId to avoid double-counting streaming events
147		// within a single API turn. When the requestId changes, the previous turn's
148		// final snapshot is flushed to the cumulative accumulators.
149		lastRequestID   string
150		pendingSnapshot *TokenSnapshot // latest snapshot for current requestId; flushed on ID change
151	
152		// lastWasUserInterrupt tracks whether the most recent user event was
153		// an ESC cancellation (the exact "[Request interrupted by user]" text
154		// marker — NOT the "for tool use" suffix variant).
155		lastWasUserInterrupt bool
156	
157		// lastWasToolDenial tracks whether the most recent user event was a
158		// tool denial ("[Request interrupted by user for tool use]" marker).
159		// Kept distinct from lastWasUserInterrupt so the cancellation rule
160		// only fires on real ESC, not on denials (which don't end the turn).
161		lastWasToolDenial bool
162	
163		// lastCWD tracks the most recent working directory seen in transcript lines.
164		lastCWD string
165	
166		// lastAssistantText holds the text content of the most recent assistant
167		// message, truncated to ~200 characters.
168		lastAssistantText string
169	}
170	
171	// NewTranscriptTailer creates a new tailer for the given transcript path.
172	// The parser handles format-specific line parsing; adapter is used for model
173	// config fallback.
174	func NewTranscriptTailer(path string, parser TranscriptParser, adapter string) *TranscriptTailer {
175		return &TranscriptTailer{
176			path:          path,
177			lastOffset:    0,
178			capacityMgr:   capacity.DefaultCapacityManager(),
179			parser:        parser,
180			adapter:       adapter,
181			openToolCalls: make(map[string]string),
182			metrics: &SessionMetrics{
183				MessageHistory: make([]MessageEvent, 0),
184				SessionStartAt: time.Time{},
185			},
186			windowSize: 60 * time.Second,
187		}
188	}
189	
190	// SetSessionStartTime allows preserving the session start time across multiple invocations
191	func (t *TranscriptTailer) SetSessionStartTime(startTime time.Time) {
192		if t.metrics != nil {
193			t.metrics.SessionStartAt = startTime
194		}
195	}
196	
197	// TailAndProcess reads the last ~64KB of transcript and processes new entries
198	func (t *TranscriptTailer) TailAndProcess() (*SessionMetrics, error) {
199		file, err := os.Open(t.path)
200		if err != nil {
201			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open transcript: %w", err)
202		}
203		defer file.Close()
204	
205		stat, err := file.Stat()
206		if err != nil {
207			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to stat transcript: %w", err)
208		}
209		fileSize := stat.Size()
210	
211		const maxTailSize = 64 * 1024
212		startPos := int64(0)
213		switch {
214		case fileSize < t.lastOffset:
215			// File rotated/truncated — reset cumulative accumulators to avoid
216			// double-counting tokens from the previous file.
217			startPos = 0
218			t.cumInputTokens = 0
219			t.cumOutputTokens = 0
220			t.cumCacheReadTokens = 0
221			t.cumCacheCreationTokens = 0
222			t.lastRequestID = ""
223			t.pendingSnapshot = nil
224		case t.lastOffset > 0:
225			// Normal incremental path: never skip ahead of the last processed byte.
226			startPos = t.lastOffset
227		case fileSize > maxTailSize:
228			// Initial read for large files: only tail the latest window.
229			startPos = fileSize - maxTailSize
230		}
231	
232		_, err = file.Seek(startPos, io.SeekStart)
233		if err != nil {
234			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to seek transcript: %w", err)
235		}
236	
237		currentOffset := startPos
238		var reader io.Reader = file
239	
240		// On the initial truncated read of a large file, we may start in the
241		// middle of a JSON line. If so, discard the partial line to align scanner
242		// to a full JSONL entry boundary.
243		if t.lastOffset == 0 && startPos > 0 {
244			prev := []byte{0}
245			if _, err := file.ReadAt(prev, startPos-1); err == nil && prev[0] != '\n' {
246				br := bufio.NewReader(file)
247				if discarded, err := br.ReadString('\n'); err == nil {
248					currentOffset += int64(len(discarded))
249				} else {
250					return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to align transcript boundary: %w", err)
251				}
252				reader = br
253			}
254		}
255	
256		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(reader)
257		// Large tool results (especially from Pi/Codex read/bash output) can exceed
258		// bufio.Scanner's 64KB default token size.
259		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 2*1024*1024)
260	
261		for scanner.Scan() {
262			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
263			currentOffset += int64(len(scanner.Bytes()) + 1)
264	
265			if line == "" {
266				continue
267			}
268	
269			// Quick JSON check.
270			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "{") || !strings.HasSuffix(line, "}") {
271				continue
272			}
273	
274			var raw map[string]interface{}
275			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &raw); err != nil {
276				continue
277			}
278	
279			// Delegate to format-specific parser.
280			parsed := t.parser.ParseLine(raw)
281			if parsed == nil || parsed.Skip {
282				// Even for skipped events, apply metadata that the parser extracted
283				// (e.g. model from model_change, CWD from session header).
284				if parsed != nil {
285					t.applyMetadata(parsed)
286				}
287				continue
288			}
289	
290			// Apply tool tracking deltas from the parser. openToolCalls is an
291			// id-keyed map — tool_use events insert by ID (idempotent: duplicate
292			// IDs from multi-line splits overwrite), tool_result events delete by
293			// ID (orphan IDs with no matching entry are harmless no-ops). This
294			// eliminates the FIFO's structural weakness where out-of-order or
295			// orphan results could pop unrelated entries. See issue #117.
296			for _, tu := range parsed.ToolUses {
297				if tu.ID != "" {
298					t.openToolCalls[tu.ID] = tu.Name
299				}
300			}
301			for _, id := range parsed.ToolResultIDs {
302				delete(t.openToolCalls, id)
303			}
304			if parsed.ClearToolNames && len(parsed.ToolResultIDs) == 0 {
305				t.openToolCalls = make(map[string]string)
306			}
307			// turn_done is Claude Code's authoritative end-of-turn signal. By
308			// definition every non-Agent tool_use opened during the turn has
309			// already received its tool_result, so anything still in
310			// openToolCalls is a stale leak. Sweeping here lets the classifier
311			// see HasOpenToolCall=false and transition working → ready.
312			//
313			// Agent tool calls are preserved: a sub-agent spawned via the Agent
314			// tool can still be running when the parent's turn_done fires
315			// (session.go:IsAgentDone treats open tool calls as authoritative
316			// over turn_done for exactly this reason), and InferSubagents relies
317			// on Agent entries in LastOpenToolNames to count in-process
318			// sub-agents. Only non-Agent leaks are swept.
319			if parsed.EventType == "turn_done" && len(t.openToolCalls) > 0 {
320				for id, name := range t.openToolCalls {
321					if name != "Agent" {
322						delete(t.openToolCalls, id)
323					}
324				}
325			}
326			// IsUserInterrupt and IsToolDenial each set their own sticky flag;
327			// any subsequent user event that isn't itself the same kind clears
328			// it. The two flags are tracked independently because only ESC
329			// feeds the classifier's cancellation rule — denials are recorded
330			// for observability but don't end the agent's turn.
331			// parsed.IsError is for tool_result errors — not used by the
332			// classifier, so we don't track it.
333			if parsed.IsUserInterrupt {
334				t.lastWasUserInterrupt = true
335			} else if IsUserEventType(parsed.EventType) {
336				t.lastWasUserInterrupt = false
337			}
338			if parsed.IsToolDenial {
339				t.lastWasToolDenial = true
340			} else if IsUserEventType(parsed.EventType) {
341				t.lastWasToolDenial = false
342			}
343	
344			// Apply metadata.
345			t.applyMetadata(parsed)
346	
347			// Track assistant text.
348			if parsed.AssistantText != "" {
349				t.lastAssistantText = parsed.AssistantText
350			}
351			if parsed.ClearToolNames {
352				t.lastAssistantText = ""
353			}
354	
355			// Accumulate content chars.
356			t.contentChars += parsed.ContentChars
357	
358			t.addMessageEvent(MessageEvent{
359				Timestamp: parsed.Timestamp,
360				EventType: parsed.EventType,
361			})
362		}
363	
364		t.lastOffset = currentOffset
365	
366		// Compute current metrics.
367		t.computeMetrics()
368	
369		// Model config fallback.
370		if t.metrics.ModelName == "" {
371			if defaultModel := getDefaultModelFromConfig(t.adapter); defaultModel != "" {
372				t.metrics.ModelName = defaultModel
373			}
374		}
375	
376		// Estimate tokens from content chars when no explicit token data exists.
377		if t.metrics.TotalTokens == 0 && t.contentChars > 0 && t.capacityMgr != nil && t.metrics.ModelName != "" {
378			cap := t.capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity(t.metrics.ModelName)
379			ratio := cap.CharToTokenRatio
380			if ratio <= 0 {
381				ratio = 4.0
382			}
383			t.metrics.TotalTokens = int64(float64(t.contentChars) / ratio)
384			if t.inputTokens == 0 && t.outputTokens == 0 {
385				t.inputTokens = t.metrics.TotalTokens * 9 / 10
386				t.outputTokens = t.metrics.TotalTokens - t.inputTokens
387			}
388		}
389	
390		// Recompute cost if token estimation filled in the breakdown but no
391		// cumulative data is available yet (char-based fallback only).
392		if t.metrics.EstimatedCostUSD == 0 && t.inputTokens > 0 &&
393			t.cumInputTokens == 0 && t.cumOutputTokens == 0 && t.pendingSnapshot == nil &&
394			t.capacityMgr != nil && t.metrics.ModelName != "" {
395			t.metrics.InputTokens = t.inputTokens
396			t.metrics.OutputTokens = t.outputTokens
397			t.metrics.EstimatedCostUSD = t.capacityMgr.EstimateCostUSD(
398				t.metrics.ModelName, t.inputTokens, t.outputTokens, t.cacheReadTokens, t.cacheCreationTokens)
399		}
400	
401		t.computeContextUtilization()
402	
403		return t.metrics, scanner.Err()
404	}
405	
406	// applyMetadata applies model/token/CWD/permission metadata from a parsed event.
407	func (t *TranscriptTailer) applyMetadata(parsed *ParsedEvent) {
408		if parsed.ModelName != "" {
409			if strings.Contains(parsed.ModelName, "[1m]") {
410				t.contextWindowOverride = 1000000
411			}
412			t.metrics.ModelName = NormalizeModelName(parsed.ModelName)
413		}
414		if parsed.ContextWindow > 0 {
415			t.contextWindowOverride = parsed.ContextWindow
416		}
417		if parsed.Tokens != nil {
418			if parsed.Tokens.Total > 0 {
419				t.metrics.TotalTokens = parsed.Tokens.Total
420			}
421			// Snapshot fields — always overwritten with latest turn for context utilization.
422			if parsed.Tokens.Input > 0 || parsed.Tokens.Output > 0 {
423				t.inputTokens = parsed.Tokens.Input
424				t.outputTokens = parsed.Tokens.Output
425				t.cacheReadTokens = parsed.Tokens.CacheRead
426				t.cacheCreationTokens = parsed.Tokens.CacheCreation
427			}
428		}
429	
430		// Cumulative token accumulation for cost calculation.
431		if parsed.CumulativeTokens != nil {
432			// Codex-style: authoritative cumulative total provided directly.
433			t.cumInputTokens = parsed.CumulativeTokens.Input
434			t.cumOutputTokens = parsed.CumulativeTokens.Output
435			t.cumCacheReadTokens = parsed.CumulativeTokens.CacheRead
436			t.cumCacheCreationTokens = parsed.CumulativeTokens.CacheCreation
437			t.pendingSnapshot = nil
438		} else if parsed.Tokens != nil && parsed.RequestID != "" {
439			// Claude Code-style: deduplicate by requestId — multiple streaming
440			// events share the same requestId within one API turn; only the final
441			// event's tokens should be counted.
442			if parsed.RequestID != t.lastRequestID && t.lastRequestID != "" && t.pendingSnapshot != nil {
443				// Flush previous turn's final snapshot to accumulators.
444				t.cumInputTokens += t.pendingSnapshot.Input
445				t.cumOutputTokens += t.pendingSnapshot.Output
446				t.cumCacheReadTokens += t.pendingSnapshot.CacheRead
447				t.cumCacheCreationTokens += t.pendingSnapshot.CacheCreation
448			}
449			t.pendingSnapshot = parsed.Tokens
450			t.lastRequestID = parsed.RequestID
451		} else if parsed.Tokens != nil && (parsed.Tokens.Input > 0 || parsed.Tokens.Output > 0) {
452			// No requestId (Pi-style): accumulate directly, no dedup needed.
453			t.cumInputTokens += parsed.Tokens.Input
454			t.cumOutputTokens += parsed.Tokens.Output
455			t.cumCacheReadTokens += parsed.Tokens.CacheRead
456			t.cumCacheCreationTokens += parsed.Tokens.CacheCreation
457		}
458		if parsed.CWD != "" {
459			t.lastCWD = parsed.CWD
460		}
461		if parsed.PermissionMode != "" {
462			t.metrics.PermissionMode = parsed.PermissionMode
463		}
464	}
465	
466	// addMessageEvent adds a new message event and maintains sliding window.
467	// Tool call counting is NOT done here — it's handled from ParsedEvent deltas
468	// in TailAndProcess to avoid double-counting.
469	func (t *TranscriptTailer) addMessageEvent(event MessageEvent) {
470		t.metrics.MessageHistory = append(t.metrics.MessageHistory, event)
471		t.metrics.LastMessageAt = event.Timestamp
472		t.metrics.LastEventType = event.EventType
473	
474		if t.metrics.SessionStartAt.IsZero() || event.Timestamp.Before(t.metrics.SessionStartAt) {
475			t.metrics.SessionStartAt = event.Timestamp
476		}
477	}
478	
479	// computeMetrics calculates messages per minute and elapsed time
480	func (t *TranscriptTailer) computeMetrics() {
481		if len(t.metrics.MessageHistory) == 0 {
482			t.metrics.MessagesPerMinute = 0
483			t.metrics.ElapsedSeconds = 0
484			t.metrics.TotalEventCount = 0
485			t.metrics.RecentEventCount = 0
486			t.metrics.RecentEventWindowStart = time.Time{}
487			return
488		}
489	
490		currentTime := time.Now()
491		latestTime := t.metrics.LastMessageAt
492		if latestTime.IsZero() {
493			latestTime = currentTime
494		}
495	
496		if !t.metrics.SessionStartAt.IsZero() {
497			t.metrics.ElapsedSeconds = int64(latestTime.Sub(t.metrics.SessionStartAt).Seconds())
498		}
499	
500		t.metrics.TotalEventCount = int64(len(t.metrics.MessageHistory))
501	
502		fiveMinutesAgo := currentTime.Add(-5 * time.Minute)
503		windowStart := fiveMinutesAgo
504		if t.metrics.SessionStartAt.After(fiveMinutesAgo) {
505			windowStart = t.metrics.SessionStartAt
506		}
507		t.metrics.RecentEventWindowStart = windowStart
508	
509		recentEventCount := int64(0)
510		for _, msg := range t.metrics.MessageHistory {
511			if msg.Timestamp.After(windowStart) || msg.Timestamp.Equal(windowStart) {
512				recentEventCount++
513			}
514		}
515		t.metrics.RecentEventCount = recentEventCount
516	
517		// Open tool calls are derived directly from the id-keyed map — the only
518		// source of truth. See the openToolCalls field comment for history (#102,
519		// #114, #117).
520		openCalls := len(t.openToolCalls)
521		t.metrics.OpenToolCallCount = openCalls
522		t.metrics.HasOpenToolCall = openCalls > 0
523		names := make([]string, 0, openCalls)
524		for _, name := range t.openToolCalls {
525			names = append(names, name)
526		}
527		t.metrics.LastOpenToolNames = names
528		t.metrics.LastWasUserInterrupt = t.lastWasUserInterrupt
529		t.metrics.LastWasToolDenial = t.lastWasToolDenial
530		t.metrics.LastCWD = t.lastCWD
531		t.metrics.LastAssistantText = t.lastAssistantText
532	
533		// Token snapshot (latest turn — for context utilization display).
534		t.metrics.InputTokens = t.inputTokens
535		t.metrics.OutputTokens = t.outputTokens
536		t.metrics.CacheReadTokens = t.cacheReadTokens
537		t.metrics.CacheCreationTokens = t.cacheCreationTokens
538	
539		// Cumulative tokens (sum of all turns — for cost calculation).
540		// Include the unflushed pendingSnapshot from the current/final requestId.
541		effectiveCumInput := t.cumInputTokens
542		effectiveCumOutput := t.cumOutputTokens
543		effectiveCumCacheRead := t.cumCacheReadTokens
544		effectiveCumCacheCreate := t.cumCacheCreationTokens
545		if t.pendingSnapshot != nil {
546			effectiveCumInput += t.pendingSnapshot.Input
547			effectiveCumOutput += t.pendingSnapshot.Output
548			effectiveCumCacheRead += t.pendingSnapshot.CacheRead
549			effectiveCumCacheCreate += t.pendingSnapshot.CacheCreation
550		}
551		t.metrics.CumInputTokens = effectiveCumInput
552		t.metrics.CumOutputTokens = effectiveCumOutput
553		t.metrics.CumCacheReadTokens = effectiveCumCacheRead
554		t.metrics.CumCacheCreationTokens = effectiveCumCacheCreate
555	
556		if t.capacityMgr != nil && t.metrics.ModelName != "" {
557			t.metrics.EstimatedCostUSD = t.capacityMgr.EstimateCostUSD(
558				t.metrics.ModelName, effectiveCumInput, effectiveCumOutput,
559				effectiveCumCacheRead, effectiveCumCacheCreate)
560		}
561	
562		// Sliding window for messages per minute.
563		legacyWindowStart := latestTime.Add(-t.windowSize)
564		messageCount := 0
565		filteredHistory := make([]MessageEvent, 0, len(t.metrics.MessageHistory))
566		for _, msg := range t.metrics.MessageHistory {
567			if msg.Timestamp.After(legacyWindowStart) || msg.Timestamp.Equal(legacyWindowStart) {
568				filteredHistory = append(filteredHistory, msg)
569				messageCount++
570			}
571		}
572		t.metrics.MessageHistory = filteredHistory
573	
574		if messageCount > 0 {
575			if len(filteredHistory) > 1 {
576				timeSpan := latestTime.Sub(filteredHistory[0].Timestamp)
577				if timeSpan > 0 {
578					t.metrics.MessagesPerMinute = float64(messageCount) / timeSpan.Minutes()
579				} else {
580					t.metrics.MessagesPerMinute = float64(messageCount)
581				}
582			} else {
583				t.metrics.MessagesPerMinute = float64(messageCount) / t.windowSize.Minutes()
584			}
585		} else {
586			t.metrics.MessagesPerMinute = 0
587		}
588	}
589	
590	// GetMetrics returns current computed metrics
591	func (t *TranscriptTailer) GetMetrics() *SessionMetrics {
592		if t.metrics == nil {
593			return &SessionMetrics{}
594		}
595		return t.metrics
596	}
597	
598	// ResetOffset resets the file offset and cumulative cost accumulators
599	// (useful for testing or file rotation).
600	func (t *TranscriptTailer) ResetOffset() {
601		t.lastOffset = 0
602		t.cumInputTokens = 0
603		t.cumOutputTokens = 0
604		t.cumCacheReadTokens = 0
605		t.cumCacheCreationTokens = 0
606		t.lastRequestID = ""
607		t.pendingSnapshot = nil
608	}
609	
610	// computeContextUtilization calculates context utilization percentage and pressure level.
611	func (t *TranscriptTailer) computeContextUtilization() {
612		if t.metrics.TotalTokens == 0 || t.metrics.ModelName == "" {
613			t.metrics.ContextUtilization = 0.0
614			t.metrics.PressureLevel = "unknown"
615			return
616		}
617	
618		var effectiveContextWindow int64
619	
620		if t.contextWindowOverride > 0 {
621			effectiveContextWindow = t.contextWindowOverride
622		}
623	
624		if effectiveContextWindow <= 0 && t.capacityMgr != nil {
625			cap := t.capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity(t.metrics.ModelName)
626			if ctx1m, ok := cap.BetaFeatures["context_1m"]; ok && ctx1m > 0 {
627				effectiveContextWindow = ctx1m
628			} else if cap.ContextWindow > 0 {
629				effectiveContextWindow = cap.ContextWindow
630			}
631		}
632	
633		// Unknown model: no context window data available — report raw tokens only.
634		if effectiveContextWindow <= 0 {
635			t.metrics.ContextWindow = 0
636			t.metrics.ContextUtilization = 0
637			t.metrics.PressureLevel = "unknown"
638			return
639		}
640	
641		utilizationPercentage := (float64(t.metrics.TotalTokens) / float64(effectiveContextWindow)) * 100
642	
643		pressureLevel := "safe"
644		if utilizationPercentage >= 90 {
645			pressureLevel = "critical"
646		} else if utilizationPercentage >= 80 {
647			pressureLevel = "warning"
648		} else if utilizationPercentage >= 60 {
649			pressureLevel = "caution"
650		}
651	
652		t.metrics.ContextWindow = effectiveContextWindow
653		t.metrics.ContextUtilization = utilizationPercentage
654		t.metrics.PressureLevel = pressureLevel
655	}
656	
657	// --- Model config fallback ---
658	
659	// getDefaultModelFromConfig reads the default model from the appropriate config
660	// based on adapter name.
661	func getDefaultModelFromConfig(adapter string) string {
662		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
663		if err != nil {
664			return ""
665		}
666		switch adapter {
667		case "pi":
668			return getPiModel(homeDir)
669		case "codex":
670			return getCodexModel(homeDir)
671		default:
672			return getClaudeModel(homeDir)
673		}
674	}
675	
676	func getClaudeModel(homeDir string) string {
677		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "settings.json"))
678		if err != nil {
679			return ""
680		}
681		var settings map[string]interface{}
682		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &settings); err != nil {
683			return ""
684		}
685		if model, ok := settings["model"].(string); ok {
686			return NormalizeModelName(model)
687		}
688		return ""
689	}
690	
691	func getCodexModel(homeDir string) string {
692		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".codex", "config.toml"))
693		if err != nil {
694			return ""
695		}
696		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(data), "\n") {
697			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
698			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "model") && strings.Contains(line, "=") {
699				parts := strings.SplitN(line, "=", 2)
700				if strings.TrimSpace(parts[0]) == "model" {
701					model := strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(parts[1]), "\"'")
702					if model != "" {
703						return model
704					}
705				}
706			}
707		}
708		return ""
709	}
710	
711	func getPiModel(homeDir string) string {
712		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".pi", "agent", "settings.json"))
713		if err != nil {
714			return ""
715		}
716		var settings map[string]interface{}
717		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &settings); err != nil {
718			return ""
719		}
720		if model, ok := settings["defaultModel"].(string); ok {
721			return NormalizeModelName(model)
722		}
723		return ""
724	}
